setting.note: An elderly man, Saleh Omar, arrives in England seeking asylum, carrying the silence of a lifetime and a feud that reaches back to Zanzibar, over a house, a debt and the son of the man he ruined. Gurnah roots the past in the real Stone Town of Zanzibar: the merchant's shop and the contested house, the harbour and its dhow trade on the Indian Ocean, and the revolution that swept it all away; the English seaside town where the two exiles finally meet lies far outside that lost world.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Abdulrazak Gurnah, first published 2001. Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: By the Sea, Abdulrazak Gurnah (2001). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The blue of the Indian Ocean that borders Zanzibar and the exile's later sea, the water the whole book of memory and departure is by. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
